body:
Title: CRA Schedule 6 2025 Canada Workers Benefit.

Sources:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-s6/5000-s6-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5009-s6/5009-s6-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5005-s6/5005-s6-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-s6/5014-s6-25e.txt

CRA Schedule 6, Canada Workers Benefit, 2025 calculates the Canada Workers Benefit refundable credit for line 45300 and advanced Canada Workers Benefit payments for line 41500 of the 2025 T1 return. The standard Schedule 6 states that Alberta, Quebec, and Nunavut residents use their custom Schedule 6 amounts instead of the standard amounts.

For standard jurisdictions, line 17 subtracts $3,000 from working income for the basic amount and line 19 uses 27%, encoded as 0.27. Line 21 maximum basic CWB is $1,633 with no eligible spouse or eligible dependant, or $2,813 with an eligible spouse or eligible dependant. Step 2 allows a secondary-earner exemption up to $16,386. Line 24 threshold is $26,855 for no eligible spouse or dependant, or $30,639 with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 26 uses 15%, encoded as 0.15. Line 30 subtracts $1,150 from working income for the disability supplement, line 32 uses 27%, encoded as 0.27, and line 34 maximum disability supplement is $843. Line 36 threshold is $37,740 unless the eligible spouse is also eligible for the disability tax credit, in which case the paired-spouse table threshold is $49,389. Line 38 uses 15%, encoded as 0.15, or 7.5%, encoded as 0.075, when the eligible spouse is also eligible for the disability tax credit.

For Alberta Schedule 6 2025, line 17 base amount is $2,760 and line 19 uses 21%, encoded as 0.21. Line 21 maximum basic CWB is $1,750 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $2,619 with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 24 threshold is $26,159 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $32,772 with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 26 uses 15%, encoded as 0.15. Line 30 disability base amount is $910, line 32 uses 26%, encoded as 0.26, line 34 maximum remains $843, and line 36 threshold is $37,826 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $50,232 with an eligible spouse or dependant.

For Quebec Schedule 6 2025, line 17 base amount is $3,600 with an eligible spouse or both spouse and dependant, otherwise $2,400. Line 19 uses 37.3%, encoded as 0.373, unless the claimant had an eligible dependant without a spouse, where it uses 20%, encoded as 0.20, or both spouse and dependant, where it uses 23.9%, encoded as 0.239. Line 21 maximum basic CWB amounts are $3,812.06 for single, $5,943.38 for spouse only, $2,044.00 for dependant only, and $3,808.23 for spouse and dependant. Line 24 thresholds are $14,170.05 for single, $21,787.19 for spouse only, $14,341.56 for dependant only, and $22,007.75 for spouse and dependant. Line 26 uses 20%, encoded as 0.20. Line 30 disability base amount is $1,200. Line 32 disability phase-in is 40%, encoded as 0.40, without an eligible spouse or dependant, and 20%, encoded as 0.20, with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 34 maximum disability supplement is $851.31. Line 36 disability thresholds are $33,230.35 for single, $51,504.09 for spouse only, $24,561.56 for dependant only, and $41,048.90 for spouse and dependant. Line 38 uses 20%, encoded as 0.20, or 10%, encoded as 0.10, when the eligible spouse is also eligible for the disability tax credit.

For Nunavut Schedule 6 2025, line 17 base amount is $6,000 and line 19 uses 14%, encoded as 0.14. Line 21 maximum basic CWB is $1,621 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $2,435 with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 24 threshold is $26,832 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $36,927 with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 26 uses 4%, encoded as 0.04, without an eligible spouse or dependant, or 8%, encoded as 0.08, with an eligible spouse or dependant. Line 30 disability base amount is $4,800, line 32 uses 26%, encoded as 0.26, line 34 maximum disability supplement is $936, line 36 threshold is $67,357 without an eligible spouse or dependant, or $67,365 with an eligible spouse or dependant, and line 38 uses 12%, encoded as 0.12, or 6%, encoded as 0.06, when the eligible spouse is also eligible for the disability tax credit.

Line 40 is the disability supplement after reduction. Line 43 is the Canada Workers Benefit amount to enter on line 45300. Line 44 and related RC210 lines calculate the advanced Canada Workers Benefit amount received, which is entered on line 41500 of the return.
heading: CRA Schedule 6 2025 Canada Workers Benefit
